title: Hackelia brachycentra
taxonomy: Hackelia brachycentra is classified within the family Boraginaceae, which includes plants such as forget-me-nots and heliotropes. This plant is a member of the genus Hackelia, which comprises approximately 25 to 30 species of flowering plants. Hackelia brachycentra is native to the mountainous regions of the western United States and is also found in parts of Canada. The plant's classification is further narrowed down to the order Lamiales, which includes a diverse group of flowering plants. Overall, Hackelia brachycentra's classification reflects its unique characteristics and evolutionary history within the plant kingdom.

intro: Hackelia brachycentra, also known as Western Stickseed, is a perennial plant native to the western regions of North America. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including fever, respiratory problems, and gastrointestinal issues, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Hackelia brachycentra include sesquiterpene lactones, alkaloids, and flavonoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Hackelia brachycentra is often prepared as a tea or tincture to treat coughs, colds, and other respiratory issues, as well as to support the digestive system and alleviate fever.
